findings.title: Reflected XSS via script tag injection
findings.description: The payload is directly inserted into the HTML without encoding, making it executable in a browser context.
findings.evidence: Payload '<script>alert('XSS')</script>' is reflected unencoded in the HTML body within an <h1> tag, allowing script execution.
findings.llm_reasoning: The payload is directly inserted into the HTML without encoding, making it executable in a browser context.

findings.title: SQL Injection vulnerability detected via error-based response
findings.description: The payload caused a 500 error with an SQL syntax error message ('near "onmouseover": syntax error'), indicating that the input is being processed in an SQL query without proper sanitization, leading to SQL injection.
findings.llm_reasoning: The payload caused a 500 error with an SQL syntax error message ('near "onmouseover": syntax error'), indicating that the input is being processed in an SQL query without proper sanitization, leading to SQL injection.
